explanation: Contradictory statements detected from the same speaker across turns
conversation_text: Hey B, I've been reading this old Plato dialogue, the Meno, where Socrates debates if virtue can be taught. What do you think? Can virtue actually be taught, like math or something? Oh yeah, Meno? That's the one with the slave boy and geometry, right? Hmm, virtue... I mean, schools teach ethics and stuff, so probably yeah? But like, can you really teach someone to be a good person? Exactly! Socrates says no, it's not teachable because no one's found a way to do it reliably. Like, all the wise guys in Athens couldn't teach their sons to be virtuous. What about that? Wait, but isn't that just because they were bad teachers? Uh, think about it—parents try to teach kids right from wrong all the time. 'Don't steal, be kind,' and some kids turn out okay. Doesn't that count as teaching virtue? Maybe, but Socrates would say that's just knowledge of what's good, not virtue itself. He distinguishes—knowing virtue isn't the same as having it. Like, I know smoking's bad, but I still do it sometimes. Virtue's more like... doing it consistently.
